lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: How goes thy quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am making progress, but I have not recovered the Grail yet.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Well, the Grail IS very elusive, it may take some perseverance.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: As I said before, speak to Merlin in the workshop by the library.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now I am a knight of the round table, do you have any more quests for me?

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Aha! I'm glad you are here! I am sending out various knights on an important quest. I was wondering if you too would like to take up this quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me of this quest.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Well, we recently found out that the Holy Grail has passed into Gielinor.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: This is most fortuitous!

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: None of my knights ever did return with it last time. Now we have the opportunity to give it another go, maybe this time we will have more luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd enjoy trying that.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Go speak to Merlin. He may be able to give a better clue as to where it is now you have freed him from that crystal.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: He has set up his workshop in the room next to the library.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may come back and try that later.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Be sure that you come speak to me soon then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am weary of questing for the time being...

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Maybe later then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe so.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: How goes thy quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am making progress, but I have not recovered the Grail yet.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Well, the Grail IS very elusive, it may take some perseverance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. King Arthur has sent me on a quest for the Holy Grail. He thought you could offer some assistance.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Ah yes... the Holy Grail...

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: That is a powerful artefact indeed. Returning it here would help Camelot a lot.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Due to its nature the Holy Grail is likely to reside in a holy place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any suggestions?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: I believe there is a holy island somewhere not far away... I'm not entirely sure... I spent too long inside that crystal! Anyway, go and talk to someone over there.

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: I suppose you could also try speaking to Sir Galahad?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: He returned from the quest many years after everyone else. He seems to know something about it, but he can only speak about those experiences cryptically.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for the advice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Sir Galahad?

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Galahad now lives a life of religious contemplation. He lives somewhere west of McGrubor's Wood I think.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Hello there adventurer, what do you want of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like some advice on finding the Grail.

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Sorry, I cannot help you with that.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: Hello there adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek the Grail of legend!

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any suggestions...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll try and find someone who does.

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Good day to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek the Grail in the name of Camelot!

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: The Grail? That is truly a noble quest indeed. None but Galahad have come close.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Galahad? Who is he?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: He used to be one of the Knights of the Round Table, but he mysteriously disappeared many years ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would he quit being a Knight?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: That is a good question.

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have the answer.

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: Good morrow!

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: I hear you are questing for the Holy Grail?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. Any hints?

lines.speaker: Sir Kay
lines.text: Unfortunately not, Sirrah.

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Hail Arthur, King of the Britons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am seeking the Grail...

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Good luck with that!

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Greetings to the court of King Arthur!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any suggestions on finding the Grail?

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: My best guess would be some sort of spell. Merlin is our magic expert. Ask him?

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Although having said that, I believe Galahad found its location once...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Know where I can find him?

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: I'm afraid not. He left here many moons ago and I know not where he went.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: You are looking for the Grail now adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: The best of luck to you! Make the name of Camelot proud, and bring it back to us.

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: Greetings! I am Sir Lancelot, the greatest Knight in the land! What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am questing for the Holy Grail.

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: The Grail? Ha! Frankly, friend, you're not in that league.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you say that?

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: You got lucky with freeing Merlin but there's no way a puny wannabe like you is going to find the Holy Grail where so many others have failed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see about that.

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest of Entrana

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Many greetings. Welcome to our fair island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I am in search of the Holy Grail.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The object of which you speak did once pass through holy Entrana. I know not where it is now however.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Nor do I really care.

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: Did you say the Grail? You are a Grail knight, yes? Well you'd better hurry. A Fisher King is in pain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I would, but I don't know where I am going!

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: Go to where the six heads face, blow the whistle and away you go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the six heads?

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: The six stone heads have appeared just recently in the world. They all face the point of realm crossing. Find where two of the heads face,

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: and you should be able to pinpoint where it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a Fisher King?

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: The Fisher King is the owner and slave of the Grail...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I will go searching.

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: Good luck with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by the whistle?

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: You don't know about the whistles yet? The whistles are easy.

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: You will need one to get to and from the Fisher King's realm. They reside in a haunted manor house in Misthalin, though you may not perceive them unless you carry something from the realm of the Fisher

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: King...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The point of realm crossing?

lines.speaker: Crone
lines.text: The realm of the Fisher King is not quite of this reality. It is of a reality very close to ours though... Where it is easiest to cross, THAT is a point of realm crossing.

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: Welcome to my home. It's rare for me to have guests! Would you like a cup of tea? I'll just put the kettle on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm on a quest to find the Holy Grail!

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: Ah... the Grail... yes... that did fill me with wonder! Oh, that I could have stayed forever! The spear, the food, the people...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: The Grail castle... It's... hard to describe with words. It mostly felt like a dream!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you leave?

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: Apparently the time is getting close when the world will need Arthur and his knights of the round table again.

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: And that includes me.

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: Leaving was tough for me, so I took this small cloth from the table as a keepsake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why didn't you bring the Grail with you?

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: ...I'm not sure. Because... it seemed to be... NEEDED in the Grail castle? Half a moment, your cup of tea is ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how can I find it?

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: I did not find it through looking - though admittedly I looked long and hard - eventually, it found me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'd better be going then.

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: Half a moment, your cup of tea is ready.

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: If you do come across any particularily difficult obstacles on your quest, do not hesitate to ask my advice.

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: I know more about the realm of the Grail than many, and I have a feeling you may need to come back and speak to me anyway...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek an item from the realm of the Fisher King.

lines.speaker: Galahad
lines.text: Funny you should mention that, but when I left there I took this small cloth from the table as a keepsake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose I could borrow that? It could come in useful on my quest.

sections.title: Attempting to talk to the Black Knight Titan again after his defeat

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, have at ye oh evil knight!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I think I'll run away!

lines.speaker: Peasant
lines.text: Woe is me! Our crops are all failing... how shall I feed myself this winter?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Hi! I don't get many visitors here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's the fishing?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Not amazing. Not many fish can live in this gungey stuff. I remember when this was a pleasant river teeming with every sort of fish...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea how to get into the castle?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Why, that's easy!

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Just ring one of the bells outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...I didn't see any bells.

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: You must be blind then. There's ALWAYS bells there when I go to the castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, well, this place is a dump!

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: This place used to be very beautiful, however, as our king grows old and weak, the land seems to be dying too.

lines.speaker: Grail Maiden
lines.text: Welcome to the Grail castle.

sections.title: Taking the holy grail before talking to the Fisher King

lines.speaker: Grail Maiden
lines.text: Welcome to the Grail castle. You should come inside, it's cold out here.

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: Ah! You got inside at last! You spent all that time fumbling around outside, I thought you'd never make it here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you know what I have been doing?

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: Oh, I can see what is happening in my realm. I have sent clues to help you get here, such as the fisherman, and the crone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seek the Holy Grail.

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: Ah excellent. A knight come to seek the Holy Grail. Maybe our land can be restored to its former glory.

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: At the moment the Grail cannot be removed from the castle. Legend has it a questing knight will one day work out how to restore our land; then he will claim the Grail as his prize.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas how I can restore the land?

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: None at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look too well.

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: Nope, I don't feel so good either.

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: I fear my life is running short... Alas, my son and heir is not here. I am waiting for my son to return to this castle.

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: If you could find my son, that would be a great weight off my shoulders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who is your son?

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: He is known as Percival.

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: I believe he is a knight of the round table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shall go and see if I can find him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you mind if I have a look around?

lines.speaker: The Fisher King
lines.text: No, not at all. Please, be my guest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, do you have a knight named Sir Percival?

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Ah yes. I remember young Percival. He rode off on a quest a couple of months ago. We are getting a bit worried, he's not back yet...

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: He was going to try and recover the golden boots of Arkaneeses.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea which way that would be?

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Not exactly. We discovered some magic golden feathers that are said to point the way to the boots...

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: They certainly point somewhere.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Just blowing gently on them will supposedly show the way to go.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Free up some space and I'll give you a feather.

sections.title: Blowing on the Magic gold feather

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: I said I will see you there then!

sections.title: Talking to Sir Percival at the Fisher Realm

lines.speaker: King Percival
lines.text: You missed all the excitement!

lines.speaker: King Percival
lines.text: I got here and agreed to take over duties as king here, then before my eyes the most miraculous changes occured here... grass and trees were growing outside before our very eyes!

lines.speaker: King Percival
lines.text: Thank you very much for showing me the way home.

sections.title: Taking more than one Holy grail

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: Wow, thank you! I could hardly breathe in there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you end up in a sack?

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: It's a little embarrassing really.

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: After going on a long and challenging quest to retrieve the boots of Arkaneeses, defeating many powerful enemies on the way, I fell into a goblin trap!

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: I've been kept as a slave here for the last 3 months!

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: A day or so ago, they decided it was a fun game to put me in this sack: then they forgot about me!

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: I'm now very hungry, and my bones feel very stiff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come with me, I shall make you a king.

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: The king of where?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father is apparently someone called the Fisher King. He is dying and wishes you to be his heir.

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: I have been told that before.

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: I have not been able to find that castle again though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I do have the means to get us there - wait, let me go get the magic whistle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I do have the means to get us there - a magic whistle!

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: Ok, I will see you there then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father wishes to speak to you.

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: My father? You have spoken to him recently?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He is dying and wishes you to be his heir.

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: I have been told that before.

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: I have not been able to find that castle again though...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I do have the means to get us there - wait, let me go get the magic whistle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I do have the means to get us there - a magic whistle!

lines.speaker: Sir Percival
lines.text: Ok, I will see you there then!

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: How goes thy quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have retrieved the Grail!

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Wow! Incredible! You truly are a splendid knight!

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Thank you for retrieving the Grail! You shall be long remembered as one of the greatest heroes amongst the Knights of the Round Table!

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: Hello there adventurer, what do you want of me?

lines.speaker: Sir Palomedes
lines.text: After your help freeing Merlin, my help is the least I can offer as a man of honour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing right now, but I'll bear it in mind. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Sir Lucan
lines.text: Congratulations on freeing Merlin!

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Hail Arthur, King of the Britons!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Hello.

lines.speaker: Sir Tristram
lines.text: Thanks for freeing Merlin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. It was easy.

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: Greetings to the court of King Arthur!

lines.speaker: Sir Pelleas
lines.text: You are a very talented Knight indeed to have freed Merlin so quickly. You have all of our gratitude.

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Sir Bedivere
lines.text: All Knights of the Round thank you for your assistance in this trying time for us.

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: Greetings! I am Sir Lancelot, the greatest Knight in the land! What do you want?

lines.speaker: Sir Lancelot
lines.text: Hmmm. I heard you freed Merlin. Either you're better than you look or you got lucky. I think the latter.

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Good day to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Know you of any quests sir knight?

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: I think you've done the main quest we were on right now...

lines.speaker: Sir Gawain
lines.text: Good day to you!

lines.speaker: Merlin
lines.text: Congratulations, brave knight, on aiding Camelot in so many ways! If we ever require help again, I will make sure to call upon you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!
